blocks:
Ayahs

أَلْهَىٰكُمُ ٱلتَّكَاثُرُ (۱)
Rivalry [and vainglory] distracted you (1)
حَتَّىٰ زُرْتُمُ ٱلْمَقَابِرَ (۲)
until you visited [even] the graves. (2)
كَلَّا سَوْفَ تَعْلَمُونَ (۳)
No indeed, soon you will know! (3)
ثُمَّ كَلَّا سَوْفَ تَعْلَمُونَ (٤)
No indeed, soon you will know for yet another time! (4)
كَلَّا لَوْ تَعْلَمُونَ عِلْمَ ٱلْيَقِينِ (٥)
Indeed, were you to know with certain knowledge, (5)
لَتَرَوُنَّ ٱلْجَحِيمَ (٦)
you would have surely seen hell [in this very life]. (6)
ثُمَّ لَتَرَوُنَّهَا عَيْنَ ٱلْيَقِينِ (۷)
Afterward you will surely see it with the eye of certainty. (7)
ثُمَّ لَتُسْـَٔلُنَّ يَوْمَئِذٍ عَنِ ٱلنَّعِيمِ (۸)
Then, on that day, you will surely be questioned concerning the Blessing. (8)

Key Points

Takathur (تكاثر) derives from kathrat (كثرت), meaning excessive seeking and boasting to others based on the abundance of wealth and children.

Manifestations of Takathur: * Takathur in polytheism and multiple gods. «ءَاَرباب متفرّقون خیرٌ ام اللّه الواحد القهّار»(129) (Prophet Yusuf (Joseph) said to his polytheist fellow prisoners: Are scattered lords better, or Allah, the One, the Irresistible?) * Takathur in food. The Children of Israel said: We cannot endure one kind of food «لن نصبر على طعام واحد». We want other foods like cucumbers, garlic, lentils, and onions. «قثائها و فومها و عدسها و بصلها»(130) * Takathur in lifespan. Just as some people wish to live for a thousand years. «یَوَدّ احدهم لو یعمّر الف سنة»(131) * Takathur in wealth. The Quran states: Some individuals seek to accumulate wealth and count it. «الّذى جمع مالاً و عدّده»(132) * Takathur in housing. The Quran, in the context of criticizing some, states: Do you build high structures on every elevation, merely playing? «أتبنون بكلّ ریع آیة تعبثون»(133) * Takathur in lust. Islam has prescribed marriage for the sexual issue and considered the enjoyment of one's spouse to be without blame: «الا على ازواجهم او ما ملكت ایمانهم فانهم غیر ملومین»(134) and has considered the gratification of lust outside of marriage to be transgression. «فمن ابتغى وراء ذلك فاولئك هم العادون»(135)

Alhakum (الهاكم) derives from lahw (لهو), meaning being preoccupied with trivial and insignificant matters that distract a person from lofty goals.

Zurtum (زُرتم) is from ziyarah (visitation), and maqabir (مقابر) is the plural of maqbarah (grave/tomb).

A dispute arose between two tribes regarding the number of their people. They decided to count them, and after counting the living, they went to the graveyard to count the statistics of the dead as well.

Imam Ali عليه السلام has statements in Sermon 221 of Nahj al-Balaghah after reciting this Surah, about which Ibn Abi al-Hadid says in his commentary: I have read this sermon more than a thousand times over fifty years, and the greatness of this sermon is such that it is worthy for the best Arab orators to gather and have this sermon recited to them, and for all of them to fall into prostration, just as they fall into prostration upon the recitation of some verses of the Quran.(136)

We recount two sentences from that sermon here: «ابائهم یفخرون» Do they boast about the graves of their fathers? «ام بعدید الهلكى یتكاثرون» Or do they seek abundance through the number of the perished? «اجساد خرّت و حركات سكنت» They were bodies that crumbled and movers that became still. «و لئن یكونوا عبراً احقّ من ان یكونا مفتخرا» Those bodies are more deserving of being a source of lesson than a source of pride.

A hadith is narrated from Imam Ali عليه السلام that He said: The first «سوف تعلمون» (you will soon know) relates to the punishment of the grave, and the second relates to the punishment of the Hereafter.(137)

Yaqin (certainty) is a heartfelt belief that is deeper than mere knowing. For example, everyone knows that a dead person cannot harm anyone, but they are unwilling to sleep next to a corpse. However, the washer of the dead sleeps comfortably next to the corpse, because people only know, but the washer has believed and reached certainty (yaqin). The way to attain certainty is by acting upon Divine commands, just as the washer of the dead reached this belief through repeated action. Based on narrations, the sign of certainty is reliance upon Allah, submission to Him, being content with His decrees, and entrusting affairs to Him.(138)

Certainty has degrees, two stages of which are mentioned in this Surah, and the other stage is mentioned in Ayah 95 of Surah Al-Waqi'ah. The stages of certainty are: Ilm al-Yaqin (Knowledge of Certainty), Haqq al-Yaqin (Truth of Certainty), and Ayn al-Yaqin (Eye of Certainty). Sometimes a person infers fire from seeing smoke; this is Ilm al-Yaqin. Sometimes he sees the fire itself; this is Ayn al-Yaqin. And sometimes he places his hand on the fire and feels its burning; this is Haqq al-Yaqin.

The meaning of seeing the Fire in «لَترونّ الجحیم» is either seeing it on the Day of Judgment, or seeing it with the Barzakhi (inter-worldly) eye and through spiritual unveiling in this world, which is more consistent with certainty (yaqin).

Based on Ahadith, the blessing (na'im) about which questions will be asked on the Day of Judgment is not water and bread, for it is unbecoming for Allah to question about such matters. Rather, the intent is the blessing of Wilayah (guardianship) and the infallible leadership.(139) Imam Reza عليه السلام said: It is unbecoming for people to question about water, bread, etc., let alone Allah. Therefore, the intent of questioning about the blessing is the blessing of the infallible leadership.(140)

Takathur is not only about population statistics and numbers; sometimes it is about wealth and children. «و تكاثر فى الاموال و الاولاد»(141) Therefore, the Quran advises that your wealth and children should not distract you. «لاتلهكم اموالكم و لا اولادكم»(142)

The Noble Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله, following the recitation of this Surah, said: «تكاثر الاموال جمعها من غیر حقها و منعها من حقها و سدّها فى الاوعیة»(143) Accumulating wealth through unlawful means, withholding its obligatory rights, and storing it in containers is Takathur.

In a poem attributed to Imam Ali عليه السلام, it is stated: O you who are preoccupied with his world, he has been deceived by the length of hope. Death comes suddenly, and the grave is the box of deeds.

129) Yusuf, 29. 130) Baqarah, 61. 131) Baqarah, 96. 132) Humazah, 2. 133) Shu'ara, 128. 134) Mu'minun, 6. 135) Mu'minun, 7. 136) Sharh Ibn Abi al-Hadid, Vol. 11, p. 153. 137) Tafsir Kabir Fakhr Razi. 138) Bihar al-Anwar, Vol. 70, p. 138. 139) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn. 140) Uyun al-Akhbar, Vol. 2, p. 129. 141) Hadid, 20. 142) Munafiqun, 9. 143) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn.

Messages

1- Excessive seeking and boasting lead man to vain and futile actions. «الهاكم التكاثر»

2- Excessive seeking is a factor in neglecting the reckoning of the Day of Judgment. «الهاكم التكاثر - لتسئلنّ یومئذ عن النعیم»

3- The scope of excessive seeking extends to counting the dead. «حتى زرتم المقابر»

4- Ignorance of the conditions of the Day of Judgment leads man astray. «الهاكم التكاثر... كلاّ لو تعلمون»

5- Warnings must be repeated in the face of deviant thoughts and behavior. «كلاّ - ثمّ كلاّ - كلاّ»

6- Through faith and certainty, man can see the future. «لو تعلمون علم الیقین لترونّ الجحیم»

7- In the culture of ignorance (Jahiliyyah), quantity and population are valued to the extent that even the dead are included in the count. «حتى زرتم المقابر»

8- Takathur (excessive seeking) and Tafakhur (boasting) carry a severe punishment. «الهاكم التكاثر... لترونّ الجحیم»

9- Considering the outcome prevents boasting. «كلاّ سوف تعلمون...»

10- One must strive to attain certainty and recognize the truths in order to be safe from the dangers of the Day of Judgment. «لو تعلمون علم الیقین لترونّ الجحیم»

11- Those who are preoccupied with the world will never reach the degree of certainty. «لو تعلمون علم الیقین» (The word law (لو) is used for impossible or hypothetical situations.)

12- Faith and certainty have degrees. «علم الیقین... عین الیقین»

13- The reckoning on the Day of Judgment is based on the resources that Allah has given to each person, and thus everyone is different from others. «لتسئلنّ یومئذ عن النعیم»
